title: Pharmacodynamics Dose-Response Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Pharmacodynamics Dose-Response Inversion: PD model fitting: estimate E_max, EC50, and Hill coefficient from dose-effect data using Emax model. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.hill.emax_model…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; inter_individual_variability_PD, time_delay_effect_site_equilibration set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: E = E_0 + E_max * C ** n / (EC50 ** n + C ** n) (sigmoidal Emax/Hill equation)
E.world_state_x: PD parameters (E_0, E_max, EC50, n_Hill) for drug effect
E.observation_y: effect measurements E_i at concentrations C_i

W.regime: Existence of the recovered PD_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); inter_individual_variability_PD dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: EC50 estimate within 2-fold for well-designed study. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |inter_individual_variability_PD|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_doses * N_params) per fit per iteration
